level1.question: What is a noun?
level1.hint: A noun names something — like boy, city, happiness, or book.

level1.question: Choose the correct verb: She ___ to school every day.
level1.hint: She is third person singular → add -s to the verb.

level1.question: What is the plural of 'child'?
level1.hint: Some nouns change completely in plural form.

level1.question: Which word is an adjective?
level1.hint: Adjectives describe or tell more about nouns.

level1.question: What punctuation ends a question?
level1.hint: Questions need a question mark at the end.

level1.question: Choose the correct pronoun: ___ is my best friend.
What is a synonym for 'happy'?
level1.hint: Synonyms have almost the same meaning.

level1.question: Choose the correct article: ___ apple is red.
What type of word is 'quickly'?
level1.hint: Adverbs tell how something is done.

level1.question: Which is a proper noun?
level1.hint: Proper nouns name specific people, places, etc. and start with capital letters.

level1.question: Fill in: I ___ my homework yesterday.
What is a conjunction?
level1.hint: Words like and, but, or join sentences or words.

level1.question: Choose the correct spelling:
level1.hint: i before e except after c.

level2.question: Which tense is used: She is reading a book now.
What is the comparative form of 'good'?
Choose the correct preposition: The cat is ___ the table.
What is a direct object?
options: Who or what does the action
level2.hint: Example: She kicked the ball. → ball is the direct object.

level2.question: Which word is an abstract noun?
level2.hint: Abstract nouns are ideas or feelings you cannot touch.

level2.question: Change to past continuous: I play football.
Choose the correct: Neither the teacher nor the students ___ happy.
level2.hint: When 'neither...nor' connects subjects, verb agrees with the closest one.

level2.question: Which sentence uses correct punctuation?
options: I like apples, bananas and oranges.
I like apples bananas and oranges.
I like apples; bananas and oranges.
I like apples: bananas and oranges.
level2.correct: I like apples, bananas and oranges.

level2.question: What is passive voice? (example)
options: The man was bitten by the dog.
level2.correct: The man was bitten by the dog.
level2.hint: Focus is on the receiver of the action.

level2.question: Choose the correct modal: You ___ finish your homework before playing.
What is the past participle of 'go'?
level2.hint: Used in perfect tenses: I have gone.

level2.question: Which is a compound sentence?
options: I like tea, but she likes coffee.
level2.correct: I like tea, but she likes coffee.
level2.hint: Two independent clauses joined by a conjunction.

level3.question: Which tense: By next year, I ___ English for five years.
level3.hint: Future perfect = will have + past participle.

level3.question: What is reported speech? (example)
options: She said that she was tired.
level3.correct: She said that she was tired.
level3.hint: Change pronouns, tenses, and time words.

level3.question: Choose correct conditional: If it ___ tomorrow, we will stay home.
level3.hint: First conditional: If + present simple, will + infinitive.

level3.question: Which is correct relative clause?
options: The boy which won is my friend.
The boy who won is my friend.
The boy whom won is my friend.
The boy that win is my friend.
level3.correct: The boy who won is my friend.

level3.question: Change to passive: They built the house in 2005.
What does 'idiom' mean?
level3.hint: Example: It's raining cats and dogs = heavy rain.

level3.question: Choose correct: Not only ___ smart but also hardworking.
What is the function of 'although'?
level3.hint: Although it rained, we went out. = contrast.

level3.question: Which sentence uses correct phrasal verb?
options: I look forward to see you.
I look forward to seeing you.
level3.correct: I look forward to seeing you.

level3.question: What is indirect object? (example)
level3.hint: Him receives the direct object (the book).

level3.question: What is a cleft sentence?
options: It was John who broke the vase.
level3.correct: It was John who broke the vase.
level3.hint: Emphasizes one part of the sentence.

level3.question: Which is correct: He is taller ___ me.
What does 'hyperbole' mean?
level3.hint: I'm so hungry I could eat a horse!

level4.question: Which tense: By the time we arrive, they ___ dinner.
level4.hint: Future perfect for action completed before another future action.

level4.question: Convert to reported speech: 'I will help you,' she said.
options: She said she will help me.
She said she would help me.
She said I will help you.
level4.correct: She said she would help me.

level4.question: Which conditional is this: If I had studied, I would have passed.
Choose correct participle clause: ___ tired, he went to bed early.
level4.hint: Feeling tired = because he felt tired.

level4.question: What is a non-defining relative clause?
level4.hint: My brother, who lives in London, is a doctor.

level4.question: Change to passive: People speak English all over the world.
options: English speaks all over the world.
English is spoken all over the world.
English was spoken all over the world.
level4.correct: English is spoken all over the world.
level4.hint: Present simple passive: is/are + past participle.

level4.question: What does 'personification' mean?
options: Giving human qualities to non-human things
level4.correct: Giving human qualities to non-human things
level4.hint: The wind whispered through the trees.

level4.question: Choose correct: Had I known, I ___ helped.
level4.hint: Inverted third conditional: Had I known = If I had known.

level4.question: Which phrasal verb means 'postpone'?
level4.hint: We put off the meeting until next week.

level4.question: What is ellipsis in grammar?
level4.hint: I can swim and (I can) dive.

level4.question: Choose correct subjunctive: It is important that he ___ on time.
level4.hint: Subjunctive uses base form after certain expressions.

level4.question: Which is correct: The number of students ___ increasing.
Choose the correct: She speaks English ___ than her brother.
What is 'alliteration'?
options: Same sound at beginning of words
level4.correct: Same sound at beginning of words

level5.question: Which structure expresses regret about the past?
options: I wish I had studied harder.
level5.correct: I wish I had studied harder.
level5.hint: Wish + past perfect for past regrets.

level5.question: Convert to cleft sentence: John broke the window.
options: It was John broke the window.
It was John who broke the window.
John was who broke the window.
The window was broken by John.
level5.correct: It was John who broke the window.

level5.question: Choose correct inversion: Never ___ such a beautiful sunset.
What is 'anastrophe'?
level5.hint: Into the valley of Death rode the six hundred.

level5.question: Which is correct mixed conditional?
options: If I had won, I would be rich now.
If I won, I would be rich now.
If I have won, I will be rich.
If I win, I was rich.
level5.correct: If I had won, I would be rich now.

level5.question: Choose the correct causative: I ___ my car serviced last week.
level5.hint: had + object + past participle = arranged for someone to do it.

level5.question: What is 'synecdoche'?
level5.hint: All hands on deck = all people.

level5.question: Which sentence uses correct ellipsis?
options: She can sing and he can too.
She can sing and he can.
She can sing he can too.
level5.correct: She can sing and he can too.

level5.question: Choose correct: The committee ___ decided to postpone the event.
level5.hint: Collective nouns like committee usually take singular verb.

level5.question: What is 'zeugma'?
options: One word used with two different meanings
level5.correct: One word used with two different meanings
level5.hint: She broke his car and his heart.

level5.question: Which is correct subjunctive mood?
level5.hint: Base form after suggest, demand, insist, etc.

level5.question: Choose the correct: Scarcely ___ the room when the phone rang.
What is 'apposition'?
options: Two nouns side by side explaining each other
level5.correct: Two nouns side by side explaining each other
level5.hint: My brother, a doctor, lives in Canada.

level5.question: Which phrasal verb means 'tolerate'?
level5.hint: I can't put up with loud music.

level5.question: What literary device is used here: The classroom was a zoo.
